mission.title: kcp: feat: add a workspace tree subcommand
mission.description: feat: add a workspace tree subcommand. Requested by 3+ users.

steps.title: Check current kcp deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kcp version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp
helm list -n kcp 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kcp installation.

steps.title: Review kcp configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kcp configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp
kubectl get configmap -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kcp
```
### Feature Description

It would be nice to have a workspace tree subcommand to visualize the hierarchy of workspaces, i.e.

steps.title: Apply the fix for feat: add a workspace tree subcommand
steps.description:
## Summary

Workspaces being nested objects its quite hard to keep mental image of the system in your head. 
Simple command to show workspace tree. Added -f to show full paths too so it is easier to use with `workspace use full_path`
```
[mjudeikis kcp]$ go run ./cmd/kubectl-workspaces/ tree -f    
```yaml
NAME           TYPE           PHASE   URL
------------- ------------- -------- -----
root           universal      Ready   ...
 ├─ org1       organization   Ready   ...
 │   ├─ team1  organization   Ready   ...
 │   └─ team2  organization   Ready   ...
 └─ foo        user           Ready   ...
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp
kubectl get events -n kcp --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "feat: add a workspace tree subcommand" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Summary

Workspaces being nested objects its quite hard to keep mental image of the system in your head. 
Simple command to show workspace tree.

NAME           TYPE           PHASE   URL
------------- ------------- -------- -----
root           universal      Ready   ...

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kcp installed or the issue environment reproducible.
